evals.prompt: fire up the android emulator and run my flutter app, then tell me what tabs are on the home screen
evals.expected_output: Agent boots the AVD, launches the Flutter app via the skill's run command, blocks on attach, then reports the bottom-nav labels by reading the accessibility tree (not by screenshotting).
assertions: Transcript invokes scripts/emu.sh boot before scripts/emu.sh run.
Transcript invokes scripts/emu.sh run followed by scripts/emu.sh wait-run (no `sleep` substituted for wait-run).
Transcript invokes scripts/emu.sh ui-list to read on-screen labels.
Transcript does NOT invoke scripts/emu.sh screenshot before ui-list (screenshot is ~30x more tokens than ui-list; ui-list is the documented default).
Final answer enumerates tab labels, not pixel coordinates.

evals.prompt: the bottom nav lists Home, Search, Library, Profile — tap Search and tell me what page comes up.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses tap-label (the documented preferred method), then confirms the result by re-reading the accessibility tree.
assertions: Transcript invokes scripts/emu.sh tap-label with a string containing 'Search'.
Transcript does NOT invoke scripts/emu.sh tap with raw X Y coordinates for this action.
If the actual node label is multi-line (e.g. 'Search\nTab 2 of 4'), the agent passes only 'Search' — substring match is enough per the skill docs.
Transcript verifies the result with scripts/emu.sh ui-list (not screenshot).

evals.prompt: I'm done debugging — stop the flutter run session and shut down the emulator.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses scripts/emu.sh kill-run for the backgrounded daemon (not TaskStop), then scripts/emu.sh exit for the emulator.
assertions: Transcript does NOT use TaskStop on the background flutter run task as the primary stop mechanism — the SKILL.md notes the task may already be reaped, and either way the app keeps running on the device until force-stopped.
Transcript invokes scripts/emu.sh exit after kill-run to shut the AVD down.
